name: Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams on autopilot

parameters.text: =✅ Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams on autopilot — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf08487-task`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Keep Valid Tasks

### Keep Valid Tasks

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Route by Work Type

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: standard, priority + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'standard' → Google Sheets · ②→ Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert · ③→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'standard' → Google Sheets

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
# Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams on autopilot

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams on autopilot. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual task routing is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Keep Valid Tasks → Route by Work Type → Process 'standard' → Google Sheets → Merge Routed Work → Stamp Processing Time → Save Execution Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating task routing in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your task routing process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
